commands.review_summary:
The PR caches tray chat markdown/tool-output inline rendering state and changes expanded tool-output cards so only the header toggles collapse.

Reproducibility: no. high-confidence live reproduction was run in this read-only Linux review. The click-to-collapse path is source-reproducible because current master wraps header and body in one Button, while the hover text-vanish path is supported by the current TextBlock/render-setter structure but was not observed directly.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human merge handling is needed for missing contributor proof, draft status, and the accessibility follow-up decision; there is no narrow automated repair request at this point.

target.title: Fix chat bubble text vanish on hover-out and tool-card collapse on click
target.body:
## Summary
  
  Fixes two bugs in the tray chat timeline (`OpenClawChatTimeline.cs`):
 
 1. **Bubble text vanishes when the mouse leaves the bubble** ‚Äî markdown `Inlines` were being rebuilt on every template-driven re-render, and the rebuild occasionally produced an empty `TextBlock`. `ApplyPlainSelectableInlines` now memoizes per-`TextBlock` via a `ConditionalWeakTable<TextBlock, string>`, so the rendered text is stable across re-renders. This applies to **both regular markdown bubbles and tool-output bubbles** (the originally reported repro and the second one the user found while testing).
 
 2. **Tool-output card collapses when the user clicks inside the body** (e.g. to start a text selection) ‚Äî the entire card was a single `Button`, so any click inside (including the expanded body) toggled it. Restructured into a **header-only `Button`** with the expanded body as a sibling. Corner rounding adjusts based on `hasExpandedBody` so the visual chrome is unchanged.
 
 Also hoists the monospace `FontFamily` into a **per-`DispatcherQueue` `ConditionalWeakTable` cache** to avoid allocating a fresh `FontFamily` on every render while still respecting `FontFamily`'s thread affinity (it is a `DependencyObject`).
 
 ## Files changed
 
 - `src/OpenClaw.Tray.WinUI/Chat/OpenClawChatTimeline.cs` (+104 / -23)
 
 Key regions:
 - Lines ~133-167: per-`DispatcherQueue` `FontFamily` cache
 - Lines ~1545-1571: Inlines cache for tool-output bubbles
 - Lines ~1639-1685: header-only `Button` restructure with `hasExpandedBody` rounding
 
 ## Validation
 
 - `./build.ps1` ‚Äî green
 - `OpenClaw.Shared.Tests` ‚Äî 2031 passed, 29 skipped
 - `OpenClaw.Tray.Tests` ‚Äî 868 passed
 - Manual UI test: both repro cases (text vanish on hover-out, collapse on click-to-select) confirmed fixed; no regression to regular text bubbles.
 
 ## Review
 
 Underwent a dual-model adversarial review (Claude Opus + GPT Codex) of the diff. Consensus hardening applied:
 - Switched from singleton `Lazy<FontFamily>` to per-`DispatcherQueue` `ConditionalWeakTable` keying (FontFamily is a thread-affine `DependencyObject` ‚Äî a process-wide singleton would crash on a second dispatcher).
 
 A follow-up a11y issue (HIGH severity, 60% confidence: tool-output body sits outside the header `Button`'s automation subtree ‚Üí Narrator may miss body content) was deferred to keep this PR scoped to the user-reported bug.
target.author: rbrid
